problem_statement: How developers can responsibly oversee and stay accountable for AI-generated ('vibe coded') code as agentic tools produce thousands of lines per day, so that speed does not sacrifice quality, security, or maintainability.
magnitude_evidence: Garry Tan (Y Combinator CEO): 'For 25% of the Winter 2025 batch, 95% of lines of code are LLM generated'; also Zuckerberg expects AI to write 50% of Meta's code by 2026 and Gene wrote 4,176 lines of Clojure in four days
insufficiency_evidence: Karpathy: 'I "Accept All" always, I don't read the diffs anymore... asking for random changes until [bugs] go away'; Humphreys: 'No, you won't be vibe coding your way to production'; Jessie Young: 'No vibe coding while I'm on call!' re: debugging in production at 2 a.m.
solution_mechanisms: use AI to document and build tests over legacy code before changing
notes: Long book excerpt, partly truncated ('...TRUNCATED FOR LENGTH...'); trade-book/practitioner conceptual work rather than empirical study, though it cites case studies (Adidas, Booking.com) and third-party stats.
